scope: Port notifier, preventSleep, and tokenEstimation service utilities onto AgenC-owned runtime primitives.

requiredBehaviors: sendNotification executes notification hooks before dispatch
preferred channels dispatch to iTerm2, kitty, Ghostty, terminal bell, disabled, or no-op outcomes
auto channel chooses a terminal-specific notification method
notification dispatch emits agenc_notification_method_used analytics metadata with configured channel, method used, and terminal name
edgeCases: channel exceptions return error instead of throwing
Apple Terminal auto mode uses the bell only when the current profile has Bell=false

requiredBehaviors: startPreventSleep increments a reference count and spawns caffeinate only for the first active macOS reference
stopPreventSleep decrements the count and kills caffeinate only when the count reaches zero
forceStopPreventSleep resets the count and tears down all timers/processes
macOS caffeinate uses -i with a 300-second timeout and a 4-minute restart interval
cleanup registration force-stops the controller on process shutdown
edgeCases: non-macOS platforms are no-op for process spawning
spawn errors and child exits clear the stored child process
restart interval refreshes caffeinate only while references remain active

requiredBehaviors: countTokensWithAPI returns 0 for empty content and otherwise delegates to countMessagesTokensWithAPI
countMessagesTokensWithAPI supports provider countTokens calls with optional VCR-style cache wrapping
Vertex countTokens calls filter betas through VERTEX_COUNT_TOKENS_ALLOWED_BETAS
thinking and redacted thinking blocks enable the minimal thinking token-count parameters
Bedrock token counting lazy-loads the runtime SDK and sends CountTokensCommand requests
Bedrock model IDs resolve foundation models directly and inference profiles through a backing-model resolver
attachment-shaped service messages are normalized before rough local estimation
edgeCases: missing provider clients or optional Bedrock SDK return null instead of throwing
tool-search-only caller and tool_reference fields are stripped for fallback API counting
fallback usage includes cache creation and cache read input tokens
